About: It is about the beginning of the Hungarian conquest of the Carpathian Basin in 895.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Árpád Feszty.
It is associated with Mór Jókai.
It is associated with the Verecke Pass of the Carpathians.
It is associated with the Hungarian press.
It is associated with the council of Budapest.
It is associated with Ignác Újváry.
It is associated with László Mednyánszky.
It is associated with Újváry.
It is associated with Spányi.
It is associated with Pál Vágó.
ComposedBy: It was composed by Árpád Feszty and his assistants.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a circular panoramic painting.
CreatedBy: It was created by Hungarian painter Árpád Feszty and his assistants.
CreatedFor: It was created for the 1000th anniversary of the event.
CreatedTo: It was created to depict the beginning of the Hungarian conquest of the Carpathian Basin in 895.
HasA: It has a length of almost 120 meters.
It has a height of nearly 15 meters.
It has hidden speakers playing realistic sound effects.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being a large cyclorama.
It is characterized by its dimensions of nearly 15 meters tall and almost 120 meters in length.
It is characterized by its depiction of the beginning of the Hungarian conquest of the Carpathian Basin in 895.
It is characterized by its completion in 1894.
It is characterized by its display in the Ópusztaszer National Heritage Park, Hungary since 1995.
It is characterized by its inspiration from a panoramic painting by Detaille and Neuville.
It is characterized by its restoration efforts.
It is characterized by its damage during the siege of Budapest in World War II.
It is characterized by its role as the main attraction of the heritage park of Ópusztaszer.
HeldIn: It was held in London for the 1899 Greater Britain Exhibition.
It was held in Budapest in 1909.
It was held in the Ópusztaszer National Heritage Park, Hungary since 1995.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by a panoramic painting by Detaille and Neuville in Paris.
IsA: It is a circular panoramic painting.
It is a painting by Hungarian painter Árpád Feszty and his assistants.
It is a depiction of the beginning of the Hungarian conquest of the Carpathian Basin in 895.
It is the biggest attraction of the millennial exhibition in Hungary.
It is the main attraction of the heritage park of Ópusztaszer.
KnownAs: It is known as Feszty Panorama.
It is known as Feszty Cyclorama.
KnownFor: It is known for depicting the beginning of the Hungarian conquest of the Carpathian Basin in 895.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Ópusztaszer National Heritage Park, Hungary.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the Verecke Pass of the Carpathians.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Hungarian conquest of the Carpathian Basin.
PartOf: It is part of the Ópusztaszer National Heritage Park.
UsedAs: It is used as the main attraction of the heritage park of Ópusztaszer.
UsedFor: It is used for commemorating the 1000th anniversary of the conquest.
UsedIn: It is used in the Ópusztaszer National Heritage Park, Hungary.
UsedTo: It is used to depict the beginning of the Hungarian conquest of the Carpathian Basin in 895.
